option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
option.value:
# importing libraries from flask import Flask from dotenv import load_dotenv import os from config import CONFIG_MAP
app = Flask(__name__) # create a flask app
# Load base .env (if present) load_dotenv()
# env files mapping env_files = { "development": ".env.dev", "production": ".env.prod", "staging": ".env.stage", }
# Determine current environment, default to development env = os.getenv("CURRENT_ENV", "development") if env not in env_files: print(f"Warning: CURRENT_ENV='{env}' not recognized; defaulting to 'development'.") env = "development"
# Attempt to load environment-specific .env file if it exists env_file = env_files.get(env) if env_file and os.path.exists(env_file): load_dotenv(env_file) else: print(f"Info: environment file for '{env}' not found ({env_file}). Using system environment variables.")
# Choose config class (safe lookup with fallback) config_class = CONFIG_MAP.get(env) or CONFIG_MAP.get("development") try: config = config_class() except Exception: config = config_class
# Resolve HOST and PORT, preferring explicit environment variables HOST = os.getenv("HOST") or getattr(config, "HOST", "127.0.0.1") PORT_raw = os.getenv("PORT") or getattr(config, "PORT", 5000)
try: PORT = int(PORT_raw) except (TypeError, ValueError): print(f"Warning: PORT value '{PORT_raw}' invalid, falling back to 5000") PORT = 5000
debug = os.getenv("FLASK_DEBUG") if debug is not None: debug = debug.lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on") else: debug = getattr(config, "DEBUG", False) or getattr(config, "debug", False)
def _print_startup_info(): print("--- Startup Configuration ---") print(f"CURRENT_ENV: {env}") print(f"Config class: {getattr(config, '__class__', config)}") print(f"HOST: {HOST}") print(f"PORT: {PORT}") print(f"DEBUG: {debug}") print("------------------------------")
if __name__ == "__main__": _print_startup_info() print(f"Starting Flask server on {HOST}:{PORT} (debug={debug})") app.run(host=HOST, port=PORT, debug=debug)
